name: Stir-Fried Scallops with Leeks
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
2 tbsp Dark soy sauce
2 ts Chili bean sauce
2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
2 ts Sugar
2 ts Sesame oil
If the scallops are very large, cut them in half. They
are cooked when they are slightly firm to the touch.
I find scallops at their best when they are just barely
cooked through.
1. Dry the scallops with paper towels and set aside.
2. Trim the leeks and discard any yellow parts.
3. Cut the leeks at the point where they begin to turn green and discard the green parts.
4. Split the white parts of the leeks in half.
5. Cut the white parts of the leeks into 2-inch segments.
6. Wash them well, several times, in cold water.
7. Heat a wok or large frying pan until it is hot, then add the oil.
8. Add the leeks, scallions, garlic, ginger, and salt to the hot oil.
9. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
10. Add the sauce ingredients and stir-fry the mixture for 3 minutes.
11. Lastly, add the scallops and continue to stir-fry for 4 more minutes, or until the scallops are just cooked.
12. Serve at once.
